articles.title: New FROST Attack Lets Websites Track What Sites and Apps You Open via SSD Timing
articles.summary: A malicious website can work out which sites you visit and which apps you open, using nothing but JavaScript and the timing of your SSD. keypoints: JavaScript-based SSD timing attack requires no permissions

analysis.title: Attack Mechanism

articles.title: One-Character Linux Kernel Flaw Enables Local Root Access, Exploits Now Public
articles.summary: Security researchers have published a detailed, working exploit for a Linux kernel use-after-free that lets an unprivileged local user escalate to root and break out of a container. The flaw, CVE-2026-23111, sits in the kernel's nf_tables packet-filtering code and was patched upstream on February 5, 2026. Exodus Intelligence released its full technical walkthrough on June 8, and it is not even

keypoints: Linux kernel use-after-free vulnerability enables unprivileged local root escalation
Working exploit publicly released; CVE-2026-23111 patched February 5, 2026

analysis.title: Critical vulnerability scope
analysis.desc: nf_tables packet-filtering flaw allows local users to escalate privileges and escape containers with root access.

analysis.title: Exploit availability timeline
analysis.desc: Upstream patch deployed February 5, 2026. Exodus Intelligence published full technical details June 8, accelerating exploitation risk.

analysis.title: Immediate threat surface
analysis.desc: Any Linux system running vulnerable kernel with local user access is exposed. Container environments face heightened breach risk.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Linux Kernel Root Exploit Now Public — Patch Immediately
ai_summary.bottom_line: If your kernel isn't patched, local attackers already own your system.
ai_summary.action_today: Linux → Terminal → uname -r, check kernel version; apt/yum update && reboot

articles.title: Check Point VPN 0-day Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild to Deploy Ransomware - CyberSecurityNews
articles.summary: Check Point VPN 0-day Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild to Deploy Ransomware  CyberSecurityNews

keypoints: Check Point VPN zero-day actively exploited for ransomware deployment
Unpatched systems vulnerable to immediate compromise and data theft

analysis.title: Active exploitation in wild
analysis.desc: Threat actors actively weaponizing unpatched Check Point VPN systems to gain network access and deploy ransomware payloads.

analysis.title: VPN as attack entry point
analysis.desc: Remote access vulnerabilities provide direct gateway to internal networks, bypassing perimeter defenses and compromising entire organizations.

analysis.title: Immediate patching critical
analysis.desc: Organizations running vulnerable Check Point VPN must apply security patches immediately to prevent active exploitation and ransomware deployment.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Check Point VPN Zero-Day: Ransomware Attacks Active Now
ai_summary.bottom_line: If your organization uses Check Point VPN, attackers are actively targeting you right now.
ai_summary.action_today: Contact your IT team: request Check Point VPN patch status and deployment timeline immediately

articles.title: Meta Blocks NSO Group's New WhatsApp Phishing Attack, Files Contempt Order
articles.summary: Meta on Monday said it detected and blocked spear-phishing attempts linked to Israeli spyware vendor NSO Group. In addition, the tech giant said it's filing a federal court contempt order against the company for violating a permanent injunction that barred it from targeting WhatsApp and its users. "They tried to trick people into clicking on malicious links to drive them to external websites

keypoints: NSO Group attempted WhatsApp phishing targeting Meta users
Meta files contempt order for injunction violation

analysis.title: Spyware vendor targets messaging platform
analysis.desc: NSO Group deployed spear-phishing attacks directing WhatsApp users to malicious external websites, attempting credential theft and device compromise through social engineering.

analysis.title: Court enforcement escalates legal pressure
analysis.desc: Meta's contempt filing challenges NSO Group's violation of existing permanent injunction, potentially triggering additional penalties and strengthening Meta's legal position against continued targeting.

analysis.title: Persistent threat despite legal barriers
analysis.desc: NSO Group continues targeting Meta platforms despite court restrictions, indicating inadequate enforcement mechanisms and highlighting the gap between legal orders and actual accountability.

ai_summary.suggested_title: NSO Group Spyware Targets WhatsApp — Meta Files Contempt
ai_summary.bottom_line: NSO is still hunting WhatsApp users despite court orders. They're not stopping.
ai_summary.action_today: WhatsApp → Settings → Account → Two-Step Verification → Enable now
